scoring: multi-dimensional, see docs/architecture-coupling/06-hotspots.md; not a single scalar

hotspots.evidence: fan-in 74 import edges / 61 importer files; 1074 LOC; 34 public symbols; median 3 symbols used per client, max 9; 5 symbols never imported externally
hotspots.root_cause: single shared utility accreted 4+ responsibilities (hook I/O protocol, state+logging, project discovery, security sanitization)
hotspots.impact: every hook rebuild/test depends on it; a bug propagates to 60+ hooks; interface change ripples repo-wide
hotspots.candidate_action: split into hooks/lib/{runtime,state,discovery,security} behind stable facade; interface segregation
hotspots.falsification_test: Experiment C: post-split, median sublibs-per-client should be 1; no client imports >2 sublibs

hotspots.evidence: top co-change pairs: plugin.json<->README 28, README<->architecture.md 24, marketplace<->README 22; commit cf32d7f 'sync the 89 Hooks header (missed by the 88->89 sweep)'; test_structure.py docstring documents prior 87-vs-88 defect
hotspots.root_cause: hardcoded 'N hooks / N skills / N agents' literals in >=9 sites
hotspots.impact: every hook addition is a multi-file coordinated change; silent drift historically observed twice
hotspots.candidate_action: generate counts from disk at build/CI time; keep test_structure count-gate as backstop
hotspots.falsification_test: Experiment F backtest: gate catches 88->89-class drift; measure FP on doc-only commits

hotspots.evidence: 32 python files reference .claude/memory paths; activeContext.md: 7 write-capable hooks + 6 readers; patterns.md: 3 writers + 6 readers; utils.file_lock used by 11 files (partial mitigation)
hotspots.root_cause: shared-database-style integration through markdown files without single owner
hotspots.impact: concurrent write races, format drift, hidden contract between 13 hooks
hotspots.confidence: VERIFIED-grep (write-detection heuristic is file-level, not per-store-statement)
hotspots.candidate_action: memory access API in lib (single writer module or mandatory file_lock + schema), ownership matrix
hotspots.falsification_test: Experiment E: concurrent-write injection on activeContext.md with/without lock discipline

hotspots.evidence: directory partition modularity ~0.00 on production graph vs Louvain Q=0.4153 with 9 stable communities (ARI 0.98-1.0 across 5 seeds)
hotspots.root_cause: package layout does not express latent domain structure (guards, knowledge/vault, memory/learning, test-integrity, security)
hotspots.impact: high cognitive load; no place to attach per-domain contracts or ownership
hotspots.confidence: VERIFIED-clustering (boundaries need semantic validation before adoption)
hotspots.candidate_action: package-by-domain inside hooks/ aligned to Louvain+DDD reconciliation (07/08 docs)
hotspots.falsification_test: Experiment A: compare graph-only vs DDD-informed grouping on semantic-clarity and cross-boundary edges

hotspots.evidence: co-change count 14; contents differ; plugin.json<->marketplace.json co-change 20-21
hotspots.root_cause: duplicated packaging metadata for plugin marketplace
hotspots.impact: drift risk in externally visible metadata
hotspots.candidate_action: single source + generation, or explicit test asserting the invariant subset

hotspots.component: 10 unregistered non-library hooks (agent_context_filter, artifact_schema_validator, doc_bridge, doc_registry, expert_registry, file_auto_parser, hook_observability, markitdown_auto_convert, model_usage_tracker, smart_model_router)
hotspots.evidence: present in hooks/ but absent from hooks/settings.json registrations; most are in pyproject coverage-omit list as 'requires full ~/.claude global environment'
hotspots.root_cause: environment-coupled or dormant code kept in the active hooks namespace
hotspots.impact: dead-code ambiguity; count metrics and docs include them; hidden runtime deps on user machine
hotspots.candidate_action: triage: register, move to attic/ (or hooks/optional/), or delete; add fitness rule 'hook file => registered or explicitly tagged'

hotspots.evidence: 88 registrations across 25 events in one file; every hook add/remove touches it plus docs plus manifests
hotspots.root_cause: single registration point (inherent to Claude Code plugin model) amplified by doc duplication (HS-02)
hotspots.candidate_action: accept (platform constraint) + schema validation test; reduce coordinated sites via HS-02 fix

hotspots.evidence: 9 python files reference ~/.claude/state, 12 reference ~/.claude/logs; coverage-omit WHY-notes: 'depend on the full ~/.claude global environment (Obsidian vault, MarkItDown, sandbox)'
hotspots.root_cause: hooks integrate with a per-user global environment that CI cannot reproduce
hotspots.impact: untestable-on-CI subset; behavior varies per machine; hidden runtime dependency
hotspots.candidate_action: environment facade in lib with injectable root; smoke-test matrix documented

hotspots.evidence: co-change README<->test_structure.py 15; test parses metadata counts across files
hotspots.root_cause: intentional fitness function (count-consistency gate) — coupling by design
hotspots.impact: positive: converts silent drift into test failure; cost: test churn on every hook add
hotspots.candidate_action: keep; reduce its blast radius by generating counts (HS-02)

hotspots.evidence: HEAD commits 4fdfe84/3f2807b 'dedup the two-scope rule copies into canonical + stub/addendum'; .claude/rules/*.md now stubs pointing to canonical
hotspots.root_cause: same rule text needed in two scopes; previously full copies drifted
hotspots.impact: was: semantic drift between copies; now mitigated by stub pattern (recent)
hotspots.candidate_action: add fitness rule: project rule file is either canonical or a stub, never a full copy

hotspots.evidence: pyproject ruff exclude with WHY-note: vendored from external repos; excluded from lint
hotspots.root_cause: third-party code imported into repo namespace
hotspots.impact: quality boundary is explicit (good), but no contract prevents repo code importing vendored code
hotspots.candidate_action: import-linter forbidden contract: hooks/scripts must not import from skills/**/scripts
